title: Congo Ebola response workers protest that they have not been properly paid
summary: BUNIA, Democratic Republic of Congo, July 9 - Ebola response workers in northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo staged protests outside three treatment centres on Thursday, saying they had not been paid all they were owed for their work.
imageAlt: Congo Ebola response workers protest that they have not been properly paid

fullArticle:
Sign up now: Get ST's newsletters delivered to your inbox

The outbreak has infected 1,759 people and resulted in 600 confirmed deaths since it was declared almost two months ago.

BUNIA, Democratic Republic of Congo – Ebola response workers in north-eastern Democratic Republic of Congo staged protests outside three treatment centres on July 9, saying they had not been paid all they were owed for their work.

The outbreak has infected 1,759 people and resulted in 600 confirmed deaths since it was declared almost two months ago, according to the latest government data released on July 8.

The World Health Organization said this week transmission was continuing of the rare form of Ebola, which kills 30 per cent to 50 per cent of those infected and has no vaccine or cure.
